sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.90 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Is it true that the automorphism group$\,F$of an infinitely generated free group$F$is a) the normal closure of a single element? b) the normal closure of some involution in$\,F$? For a free group$F_n$of finite rank$n$\ M.\,Bridson and K.\,Vogtmann have recently shown that$\,F_n$is the normal closure of some involution, which permutes some basis of$F_n$. It is also known that the automorphism group...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
